description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular, single-story agricultural building with a broad, gently sloped gable roof clad in red shingles. The structure features timber framing with visible wooden posts supporting an overhanging roof along the front, providing a sheltered area. The front facade includes large glass windows and doors, allowing natural light to enter and providing visibility into the interior. On one side, there are double wooden barn doors with black metal hinges, suitable for livestock or equipment access. The rear and side walls are clad in horizontal wooden planks, with a few rectangular windows for additional light and ventilation. The overall design is practical and robust, typical of rural or peri-urban agricultural architecture, but could be found on the outskirts of urban environments where livestock facilities are integrated.
description: A rectangular cowshed with a red gable roof, timber framing, large front windows, and double barn doors, clad in horizontal wooden planks.
description_view_0: Front view showing the overhanging roof, timber posts, large glass windows, and entry doors.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the double wooden barn doors and the continuous red gable roof.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying horizontal wooden planks and two rectangular windows.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with plain wooden siding and no visible doors or windows.
attribute_door_style: double wooden barn door with black metal strap hinges
attribute_window_presence: three rectangular windows, two on the rear and one on the front
